pattern: A doubly-linked list of frequency buckets, each holding the set of keys at that count, combined with a hash map from key to its bucket, achieves O(1) increments, decrements, and min/max queries.
hints: getMaxKey and getMinKey must be O(1), which means you cannot scan all keys to find extreme counts — the order must be maintained continuously.
Group keys by their count in a sorted sequence of buckets; inc moves a key to the next bucket and dec moves it to the previous one.
A doubly-linked list of buckets (sorted by count) lets you insert/remove a bucket in O(1) and gives constant access to the head (min) and tail (max).

edge_inputs.description: basic increment, min/max agree when only one key exists
inc then dec removes a key; querying after removal returns empty
multiple keys at the same count; min and max may return any one of them
decrement from count 2 to 1, check min updates correctly
single character key, many increments then decrements back to removal
after all keys removed and one re-added, min and max both reflect the lone key

stress.description: randomized inc/dec over a small alphabet; dec only issued when key is present
stress.note: small key space forces contention; min/max queries sprinkled throughout
